nodes.label: MATH 3A
nodes.title: Calculus with Applications, First Course
nodes.description: Differential calculus including analytic geometry, limits, derivatives, techniques and applications of differentiation; introduction to integration; logarithmic and trigonometric functions.

nodes.label: MATH 4A
nodes.title: Linear Algebra with Applications
nodes.description: Systems of linear equations, matrix algebra, determinants, vector spaces and subspaces, basis and dimension, linear transformations, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, diagonalization, and orthogonality.

nodes.label: MATH 8
nodes.title: Transition to Higher Mathematics
nodes.description: Propositional logic, techniques of mathematical proof, and fundamental structures including sets, functions, and relations. Essential preparation for upper-division mathematics major courses.

nodes.label: PHYS 7A
nodes.title: Basic Physics
nodes.description: Kinematics in 3D, Newton's laws, energy, momentum and collisions, rotation, statics and equilibrium.

nodes.label: MATH 118A
nodes.title: Real Analysis
nodes.description: Axioms of the real numbers, topology of metric spaces, numerical sequences, and infinite series.

nodes.label: MATH 111A
nodes.title: Abstract Algebra
nodes.description: Introduction to group theory for finite groups: homomorphisms, normal subgroups, quotients, direct products, group actions, and classification of finitely generated Abelian groups.

nodes.label: MATH 124
nodes.title: Partial Differential Equations
nodes.description: Wave, heat, and potential equations. (UCSB catalog: MATH 124A.)

nodes.label: MATH 132
nodes.title: Optimization
nodes.description: Global and local optimization, constrained and unconstrained optimization, convex versus nonconvex optimization, gradient methods, Newton and quasi-Newton methods, and trust region methods. (UCSB catalog: MATH 132A.)

nodes.label: MATH 160
nodes.title: Introduction to Numerical Analysis
nodes.description: Numerical methods for nonlinear equations (Newton method), integration (quadrature and composite integration), and initial value problems for ODEs (Euler and Runge–Kutta methods). (UCSB catalog: MATH 104A.)

nodes.label: MATH 170
nodes.title: Introduction to Mathematical Finance
nodes.description: Mathematical methods for asset pricing models, equilibrium and derivative pricing, options, bonds, and the term structure of interest rates; optimization models for risk management and financial engineering.

nodes.label: Data science
nodes.title: Data science
nodes.description: Career path for Applied Mathematics graduates: data science.

nodes.label: Engineering
nodes.title: Engineering
nodes.description: Career path for Applied Mathematics graduates: engineering.

nodes.label: Finance
nodes.title: Finance
nodes.description: Career path for Applied Mathematics graduates: finance.

nodes.label: Research
nodes.title: Research
nodes.description: Career path for Applied Mathematics graduates: research.
